relatedness_reasoning: Both files concern fraud/scam incidents and transaction holds; risk_and_compliance receives fraud reports from customer_services and sends transaction hold notices to accounts. Customer, transaction, and risk severity data flow logically from inbound to outbound, forming a clear compliance pipeline.

mappings.reasoning: Both are unique identifiers for compliance actions; reportId becomes the basis for noticeId generation.
reportDate marks when the fraud report was created; timestamp marks when the hold notice was generated, likely shortly after.
Hardcoded enum value 'Risk and Compliance' representing the issuing department; not derived from inbound data.
Hardcoded enum value 'Accounts' representing the destination department; not derived from inbound data.
Hardcoded enum value 'TRANSACTION_HOLD' representing the notice classification; not derived from inbound data.
Both fields represent risk severity level; inbound severity is 'high' enum, outbound is 'HIGH' enum (case transformation).
Inbound customer.accountNumber (masked account) becomes outbound accountNumber; note format differs (account_number to iban) but represents the same account identifier.
Direct copy of customer identifier from inbound to outbound.
Direct copy of customer full name from inbound to outbound.
Hold reason combines fraud description and risk indicators to explain why the transaction hold was placed.
Hold becomes effective around the time the incident was detected; detectionDate serves as the logical basis for hold activation.
No inbound field specifies hold duration; this is a compliance policy decision not derived from the fraud report.
Inbound transactionReference identifies the fraudulent transaction; becomes transactionId in the held transactions list.
Direct copy of fraudulent transaction amount from inbound to outbound.
Direct copy of currency code (GBP) from inbound to outbound.
Inbound recipientCountry (NI) identifies where fraudulent funds were sent; becomes destinationCountry in held transaction details.
Inbound transactionStatus is 'blocked'; outbound status is 'HELD' (semantically equivalent compliance state).
Risk score is computed from the array of risk indicators; no single inbound field directly provides a numeric score.
Inbound riskIndicators array becomes outbound riskFlags array; same concept, different field name.
Inbound actionsTaken describes protective actions already executed; outbound requiredActions specifies actions the Accounts department must perform (derived from incident context).
Inbound escalationRequired is a boolean flag; outbound escalationLevel is a numeric tier derived from severity and escalation flag.
Inbound escalationRequired boolean indicates need for escalation; outbound approvalRequired indicates need for senior management approval (related compliance concept).
Hardcoded enum value 'Senior Compliance Officer' representing the authorized approver role; not derived from inbound data.
Direct copy of supplementary narrative details from inbound additionalNotes to outbound notes.
No inbound field specifies when the hold notice expires; this is a compliance policy decision.
Hardcoded enum value 'automated_compliance_system_v2.1' identifying the issuing system; not derived from inbound data.
Inbound severity 'high' maps to outbound priority 'URGENT'; both indicate urgency level of the compliance action.
